title: JSX Rules and Fundamentals Recap

learningObjectives.text: Explain jsx rules using a new example and justify the result.
Identify and correct common jsx mistakes using a new example and justify the result.
Explain module recap using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns jsx rules and fundamentals recap from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Components, JSX, and Props** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **JSX rules** → **Common JSX mistakes** → **Module recap**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: JSX rules
sections.body: JSX must produce one root value, tags must close, JavaScript expressions use braces, DOM property names follow React conventions, and lists require stable keys.

sections.title: Common JSX mistakes
sections.body: Frequent errors include adjacent roots without a wrapper, unstable keys, rendering objects directly, and calling an event handler during render instead of passing it.

sections.title: Module recap
sections.body: Connect JSX, components, and props as one model: JSX describes elements, components provide reusable behavior, and props supply read-only inputs.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The fragment supplies one root and the list uses stable keys.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: JSX Rules and Fundamentals Recap connects JSX rules, Common JSX mistakes, Module recap.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
